title: Expose Facial command and receipt API parity
operator_request: Facial originally exposed many workflow commands. Handshake needs native backend/API/Argus command parity so models can use the capability without file queues or external app assumptions.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Expose commands through Handshake API/client/Argus surfaces. Do not recreate .facial/data/api file queues as the primary product API unless explicitly accepted as an import adapter.

scope.summary: Create Handshake-native command routes, status APIs, receipt readers, and frontend client methods for Facial features.
base_scope: Map Facial app command surface to Handshake-native routes and client methods.
Implement list_features, start_run, get_run_status, read_artifact, identity actions, review actions, and export actions where corresponding MT backends exist.
Return structured receipts with actor, action, status, result, artifact refs, and error/degraded state.
Make async or long-running actions recoverable and observable by parallel agents.
Keep old file-queue behavior as import/reference context only unless a compatibility adapter is explicitly needed.
acceptance_criteria: Each relevant Facial command has a Handshake-native equivalent or an explicit deferred reason.
Receipts are actor/action/status/result/error structured and include artifact refs.
Artifacts are readable by ref from the backend/client path.
Async actions have recoverable status and no stranded run state after restart/reload.

Frontend client parses the live success command envelope and rejects degraded/blocked/error envelopes until the backend emits durable failure envelopes; HTTP errors remain the current negative-path contract and MT-031 carries durable failure envelope parity.
risks_and_controls: Risk: routes exist but are not model-usable. Control: every route needs a client method or documented headless invocation and Argus-visible state if UI exists.
Risk: async run state is stranded. Control: run status must reconstruct from persisted artifacts/receipts.

Risk: receipt schemas drift between backend and frontend. Control: parser tests assert the current success-envelope contract and reject unproduced status envelopes; MT-031 carries durable degraded/error receipt parity.
verification: Route tests for feature listing, run start/status, artifact read, identity/review/export actions.
Frontend parser tests for receipts, degraded states, and errors.
Recovery test proving status can be rebuilt from persisted artifacts.

implemented: Added Handshake-native Facial feature listing and command route metadata under /atelier/facial/features.
Added ArtifactStore-backed JSON artifact read for Facial-owned L1 artifacts only, with schema allowlist and manifest/source metadata checks.
Added review session, shard claim, decision, status, montage, and export API routes over the MT-028 Facial review artifacts.
Made claim/status/montage/export recovery server-authoritative by discovering persisted Facial claim/decision artifacts from ArtifactStore and merging them with caller-provided refs.
Serialized claim creation through an in-process claim lock so parallel same-process agents cannot claim the same shard with stale or empty refs.
Added frontend backend-client request builders and parsers for Facial feature lists, artifact reads, and live success command envelopes.
Corrected route metadata to distinguish HTTP response schema from nested result artifact schema.
implementation_summary.scope_boundary: MT-029 exposes success-path command receipt parity and HTTP error negative paths. Durable degraded/error command envelopes and failure receipt artifacts are intentionally not claimed here and are captured as MT-031.

passed: git diff --check on scoped MT-029 product files
Post-hardening subagent review passed backend claim recovery/artifact-read restrictions and frontend route metadata/success-envelope parser contract.
cleanup: Removed E:/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target/wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul after it reached roughly 55 GB.
Used and removed throwaway frontend verification target E:/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target/wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul-verify.
Retained small proof logs under E:/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test for state recovery.
known_follow_up: MT-030 must expose the command APIs in the Atelier/Facial UI, Argus/debug surfaces, and internal manual.
MT-031 must add durable degraded/error command envelopes and failure receipt artifacts if models need structured command errors instead of HTTP errors.

addressed: Used narrow ArtifactStore read helper rather than exposing filesystem reads.
Made feature list distinguish implemented native routes from symbolic capabilities.
Added route-level API proof over real PostgreSQL intake data.
Added pure request builders for Facial feature, artifact, session, claim, decision, status, montage, and export commands.
Added parser tests that preserve artifact refs and reject unsupported command response states.

findings_addressed: Server now discovers persisted claim/decision artifacts instead of trusting only caller-supplied refs.
Facial artifact reader now rejects generic JSON artifacts and non-Facial schemas.
Route metadata now separates response_schema_id and result_schema_id.
Frontend parser now rejects degraded/blocked/error command envelopes because backend does not yet produce them.

manual_requirement: Required if this MT exposes or changes operator/model-visible Facial behavior.

handoff.note: MT-029 product commit pushed. Success command/API/receipt parity is implemented with server-discovered review state recovery and restricted Facial artifact reads. active_mt_id remains MT-010 because that is the separate media/linking lane. Continue operator-visible UI/manual/Argus work in MT-030 and durable failure/degraded command envelope parity in MT-031.
